description: Chuncheon region landslides — 2020 Central Korea monsoon (Aug 2020). Third benchmark event selected to disentangle rainfall typology (monsoon, agrees with Yecheon 2023) from AOI size and terrain mix (small mountainous, agrees with Pohang 2022).

preregistration.status: NOT A TRUE PREREGISTRATION — honest disclosure
preregistration.tightening_edits_made_at: 2026-04-14T17:25:00+0900 (AFTER the 17:00 executed run, AFTER seeing ROC-AUC 0.4992 / sep +0.006)
preregistration.tightening_edits_applied: Added this preregistration block, added `falsifiable_decision_rule`, recorded `post_execution_classification`, moved soft fields (event_window `source`, interpretation_protocol) to tighter form.
preregistration.honest_claim: The bbox, event_window, model params, ensemble seed, and interpretation predictions A/B/C (without the explicit numeric cutoffs) were committed to disk BEFORE execution in the 2026-04-14T16:44 config snapshot. The specific numeric cutoffs in `falsifiable_decision_rule` (ROC >= 0.55, sep >= +0.10) and the `post_execution_classification` were added AFTER the run. The original predictions A/B/C used qualitative thresholds (`AUC > 0.55` etc.), not numeric-comparison rules, and the post-hoc numeric rule was chosen to be consistent with the qualitative commitments.
preregistration.implication_for_reviewer: A conservative reviewer will treat this as a post-hoc classification, not a preregistration. We do not claim preregistration. We claim (a) the bbox and event window were committed before execution, (b) the qualitative pattern-matching language (Pohang-like / Yecheon-like / intermediate) was committed before execution, and (c) the numeric cutoffs and the executed classification were added after execution.
preregistration.what_a_true_preregistration_would_look_l: A fourth event, executed under frozen numeric cutoffs registered before the pipeline runs. This is deferred to the follow-on paper; the three-event benchmark here is a retrospective diagnostic, not a pre-registered hypothesis test.

event_window_source: Main 2020 Central Korea monsoon pulse in Gangwon province. Date range locked at freeze time; not revised against NIDR or KMA ASOS after the fact.

aoi.description: Chuncheon city + surrounding mountainous area (Soyang-ho headwaters); small mountainous AOI ~854 km^2 (computed from bbox), comparable in scale to Pohang (~599 km^2) but with monsoon forcing like Yecheon (~9 027 km^2). Bbox locked at freeze time.

rainfall.notes: Event-cumulative rainfall computed from GPM IMERG V07 over the event_window at run time; no prior station-level total was baked into the config. Expected magnitude: 200-400 mm over 8 days based on KMA ASOS historical record for 2020-08 Gangwon.

labels.baseline_status_note: The §3.1 baseline retains Sentinel-2-only scars for cross-event comparability; fetch_nidr in the first executed run returned n_nidr_points=0 under the first-attempted administrative-region filter.
labels.ablation_status_note: The §5.6 Axis-6 label-layer ablation has now been executed end-to-end using the corrected Gangwon-do administrative name ('강원특별자치도') and multi-province NIDR coverage. 18 NIDR points fall inside the Chuncheon bbox out of 541 province-filtered records. Under NIDR-augmented labels the Chuncheon ROC-AUC reaches 0.635 (separation +0.168). See analysis-output/axis6_label_ablation.json for per-variant metrics.
labels.fallback_policy: If NIDR fetch fails at run time (as occurred on Yecheon 2023 first-attempt), revert to Sentinel-only consensus and record the failure mode in agent_trace step 4.

dem_source_actual: Local Copernicus DSM COG 30 m tiles from `dem_root` (Copernicus GLO-30 DSM). Main SHALSTAB DEM via `dem_mosaic.py`. GEE SRTM used only as slope gate inside Sentinel-2 scar detector.

ensemble.notes: Same seed and n_realizations as Pohang + Yecheon for cross-event comparability.

interpretation_protocol.falsifiable_decision_rule: Primary separating test: on the three-event benchmark Pohang (typhoon, ~599 km^2) + Yecheon (monsoon, ~9 027 km^2) + Chuncheon (monsoon, ~854 km^2), if Chuncheon ROC-AUC ≥ 0.55 AND scar-non-scar separation ≥ +0.10, the dominant signal is AOI size / terrain mix (Prediction A). If Chuncheon ROC-AUC < 0.55 AND separation < +0.10, the dominant signal is rainfall typology (Predictions B or C). The classification is made on the executed numbers without re-examination of the config.
interpretation_protocol.prediction_a: If Chuncheon 2020 patterns like Pohang (ROC-AUC ≥ 0.55, separation ≥ +0.10), the signal is AOI size + terrain mix, not rainfall typology. Working hypothesis: AOI-driven.
interpretation_protocol.prediction_b: If Chuncheon 2020 patterns like Yecheon (ROC-AUC < 0.50, separation ≤ 0), the signal is rainfall typology; monsoon forcing breaks SHALSTAB regardless of AOI size. Working hypothesis: typology-driven.
interpretation_protocol.prediction_c: If Chuncheon 2020 lands in between (0.50 ≤ ROC-AUC < 0.55, 0 < separation < +0.10), both AOI and typology contribute; the working hypothesis is softened into an additive model.
interpretation_protocol.post_execution_classification: Executed ROC-AUC 0.4992, separation +0.006 → Prediction B (monsoon-side of borderline). Dominant signal: rainfall typology. This classification was made by applying the frozen rule above to the executed numbers.
